summary: Iteration 18 — Filter-propagation regression matrix (10 pages x 4 permutations). Used URL-based filter seeding (filters.jsx reads p/d/t/c/ch/cm from querystring). Captured body-text numeric fingerprints + MD5 hash of first 40 metrics on each page. RESULTS: Permutation A (Kenya + Vivo Sarit, this_month) and Permutation C (custom 2026-04-15→2026-04-20) PROPAGATE correctly on every loaded page. Permutation B (Uganda only, this_month) is INCONCLUSIVE on Overview / Locations / Footfall / Exports — the first-40 number fingerprint matched baseline (identical MD5). Needs E1 verification whether those pages truly scope to Uganda or ignore the country filter.

matrix.legend: hash = MD5 of first 40 numeric tokens on page; PASS = hash differs from baseline; FAIL = hash identical; N/A = page did not finish loading within 4.5 s (shows 0 metrics).

inventory.note: Active-filter breadcrumb + 'Include warehouse' toggle both rendered

ibt.result: INCONCLUSIVE - n=2 (sparse data, known upstream gap)

exports.result: PASS — LINES 1,301 vs baseline 5,000; NET KES 4,850,497 vs 17,981,140

overview.result: FAIL? — identical first-40-number fingerprint as baseline; numCount grew 124→200 so body did change, but the leading 40 metrics are unchanged. Investigate whether country filter actually applied.

customers.result: N/A — page didn't render data in 4.5 s wait (empty body). Re-test with longer wait.

exports.result: FAIL? — identical first-40-number fingerprint as baseline (5,000 lines cap also applies)
PASS — LINES 4,825; date range '15 Apr 2026 → 20 Apr 2026' visible in header

spec_checks.inventory_breadcrumb_on_A: PASS — element containing 'Showing inventory for' rendered under Permutation A
spec_checks.inventory_warehouse_toggle_on_A: PASS — [data-testid='inv-include-warehouse-toggle'] present under A only (not under baseline/B/C)
spec_checks.footfall_delta_conv_column: PASS — 'Δ Conv' / 'Conv (pp)' text present on baseline, A and C
spec_checks.customers_pct_new_column: PASS — under Permutation A and C the Customers page exposes a '% New' column
spec_checks.customers_churn_rate_~95.88: NOT VERIFIED — churn KPI text did not match regex 'Churn.*\d+%' within the 4.5 s render window on any permutation. Either the element name differs from 'Churn' or the /churned-customers call (known 500 upstream) hadn't returned. Recommend targeted re-test.

integration_issues.flow: Country filter propagation (Permutation B = c=Uganda, p=this_month)
integration_issues.issue: Overview / Locations / Footfall / Exports render with IDENTICAL first-40 metric fingerprint vs baseline (no filter). MD5 match is suspicious and may indicate the country-only filter (without POS/date change) is not propagating to those pages, OR the pages are showing stale-cached numbers while the Uganda query is still inflight.

integration_issues.flow: Customers / Products / Inventory / CEO under Country=Uganda
integration_issues.issue: Pages returned empty body (numCount=0) within 4.5 s wait — either slow query on country filter or the query aborted. Retest with 10-15 s wait to distinguish slow-load from broken-filter.

action_items: E1 to investigate why overview/locations/footfall/exports show identical first-40 metric fingerprint under c=Uganda vs no-country baseline. Reproduce: open /?p=this_month vs /?p=this_month&c=Uganda and compare KPI values manually. Confirm whether the country filter is piped through to the /api/kpis, /api/locations, /api/footfall and /api/orders calls (check network tab for country=Uganda query param).
Extend customers/products/inventory/ceo wait time OR prefetch on filter-change so Uganda-filtered queries finish before render. Consider a skeleton-loader so empty body doesn't look like 'no data'.
Verify Churn Rate ~95.88% cumulative KPI renders (expected on Customers page). Current test regex did not match.
(LOW) Iteration 16 suggestion still open: persist dateFrom/dateTo/compareMode in URL or localStorage so full reloads preserve filter state.
critical_code_review_comments: Strong design: URL-as-source-of-truth filter sync (filters.jsx) lets deep links carry p/d/t/c/ch/cm — crucial for reproducible BI URLs.

Potential bug in country filter path: Permutation B (c=Uganda) appears to leave Overview/Locations/Footfall/Exports values unchanged. Suspect that `channels` is falsy-checked to decide whether to propagate, while `countries` may be dropped by an early-return in the API query builder. Check /app/backend/server.py /api/kpis handler and the frontend useKpis hook's params composition for `country` vs `countries` naming drift.
Exports page /api/orders takes >10s on first hit — cache banner would help UX during that window.
/churned-customers upstream 500 is swallowed silently; Customers page should show an explicit 'Churn data unavailable — upstream error' alert instead of 0.02% fallback or empty state.

success_rate.frontend: Permutation A: 10/10 PASS  ·  Permutation C: 9/10 PASS (products didn't render)  ·  Permutation B: 0/10 fully verified — 4 pages show identical fingerprint (possible regression), 4 pages didn't render in time, 2 sparse-data pages inconclusive.

context_for_next_testing_agent: Use URL params to set filters — fastest & reliable (filters.jsx reads p/d/t/c/ch/cm on initial mount). For country-only tests extend wait to 10-15 s after goto because Uganda triggers slower upstream queries. Login selector that works: `page.fill('input[type=email]',...)` then click `form button[type=submit]` LAST (first submit on page is Google OAuth button). Token lives in localStorage under `vivo_bi_token`.
rca_of_the_issue: Permutation B (c=Uganda) fingerprint collision with baseline suggests the country-only filter may not reach the data layer. Needs: (1) DevTools Network capture on /?p=this_month&c=Uganda — inspect /api/kpis call for country=Uganda param; (2) backend log inspection for the same request; (3) compare response body hash vs /api/kpis?... without country. If response bodies differ but UI shows same numbers, the bug is in the React state update; if responses are identical, the bug is in the backend proxy forwarding the country filter upstream.
